name: Raspberry Pi
description: The Raspberry Pi is a low-cost, credit-card-sized single-board computer that runs a full Linux operating system. The latest model (Raspberry Pi 5) features a quad-core ARM processor, up to 8GB RAM, GPIO pins for hardware interfacing, camera and display connectors, USB 3.0, Ethernet, Wi-Fi, and Bluetooth. It can run ROS 2, OpenCV, TensorFlow Lite, and other robotics software, making it a capable brain for small to medium robots.
whyUseIt: The Raspberry Pi bridges the gap between simple microcontrollers (Arduino) and full desktop computers. It runs a real operating system, supports high-level languages (Python, C++), can process camera images, run machine learning inference, and communicate over Wi-Fi — all for around $80-120 AUD. It is the default choice for robots that need more computational power than an Arduino but cannot carry a full laptop, such as small mobile robots, drones, and educational platforms.
